hawkish_phrases: inflation compensation based on Treasury Inflation Protected Securities rose above their pre-pandemic levels
seven participants expected overall inflation to be above the Committee's 2 percent longer-run objective in 2023
upside risk to inflation...a stronger-than-expected recovery, coupled with the possible emergence of pandemic-related supply constraints
market expectations regarding the federal funds rate target range did not show a tightening until 2024
inflation was projected to overshoot 2 percent by a moderate amount
dovish_phrases: the ongoing public health crisis will continue to weigh on economic activity, employment, and inflation in the near term
employment continued to be well below its level at the beginning of the year
continue to increase its holdings of Treasury securities by at least $80 billion per month and agency MBS by at least $40 billion per month
until substantial further progress has been made toward the Committee's maximum employment and price stability goals
expects it will be appropriate to maintain this target range until labor market conditions have reached levels consistent with the Committee's assessments of maximum employment
summary: The FOMC unanimously held rates at 0-0.25% and introduced outcome-based asset purchase guidance ($80B Treasuries, $40B MBS monthly) until substantial further progress toward dual mandate goals, with explicit dovish forward guidance emphasizing labor market weakness and near-term pandemic headwinds despite positive vaccine news.
